title: How exactly does someone pilot an Eva
display_title: How exactly does someone pilot an Eva
wikitext:
[]: How exactly does someone pilot an Eva???             Quote Post by offthechainjoe » Sun Sep 20, 2009 12:44 pm Ok, I'm sorry if this has been asked before.... but I couldn't find anything like this and I really want to know... How exactly do the pilots in Evangelion actually pilot their Eva's??? The only controls seem to be the 2 hand levers that they can move back and forth ...With just those they make their Eva's run, grab, punch, kick, jump, flip? etc

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Sep 20, 2009 12:46 pm There sync ratio has to be good, they also have to think and concentrate on the movement.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Sep 20, 2009 12:53 pm Rebuild shows that there are several buttons on the levers, presumably for the complicated actions. I also wouldn't be surprised if there were foot pedals that we don't ever get to see. Ritsuko does mention that there's like, over 9000 different things you need to have memorized about an entry plug.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Sep 20, 2009 12:59 pm Very carefully.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Sep 20, 2009 1:13 pm People have speculated that the 'hand levers' don't actually do anything and are just something for the pilots to hold onto while they control it with their mind. We know there's a trigger for firing and in Rebuild SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 20, 2009 1:39 pm There are a number of points in the series where Rei is shown in the same posture as EVA-00; incidents with the other pilots haven't stuck in the mind so much.

[]:             Quote Post by Adekis » Sun Sep 20, 2009 1:39 pm It always seemed to me that you would move the Eva in much the same way you would move your own body, the difference being that you would also be able to move your body... So I guess it would be like having two separate nervous systems... haha... Actually, I'm not sure how the movements or the levers would fit in, I always assumed that those levers are there to increase synch ratio or something, though the fact that they have triggers seems to contradict this theory... Well, the true is I have no idea, and I doubt that Anno was really thinking " Okay, how do they pilot this thing? ". In pretty much every robot show I've ever seen (with the exception of Big O), the pilots just move around drmatically with a couple levers and buttons, and you aren't really sure what any of them do. I guess it's a possibility that that's what they were going for, not being able to tell how they do it. But part of it is definitely mental.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Wed Sep 23, 2009 9:01 am Regarding the evas mimicking the pilot's movements: In episode 22 unit-02 cringes and wobbles like Asuka is in her entry plug? And another one with Asuka is in EoE, where she is holding herself like an embryo. I think in both cases she wasnt the most focused in piloting her eva, possibly more evidence that the evas mostly copy the pilots movement's with enough sync ratio.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Thu Sep 24, 2009 11:50 am But we know from the choice of Shinji over Rei to snipe Ramiel in Op Yashima that fine-tuned movement does depend on sync ratio. Which indicates that triggers aside, movement is probably entirely thought-based. Perhaps the two frontal levers are for better control of the arms? Also, what sort of mental activity would go on in order to extend an AT Field?

[]:             Quote Post by helbrect » Fri Sep 25, 2009 10:32 am become 2 dimensional?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Sep 25, 2009 1:35 pm I'd guess that the switches on the handles are primarilly there to deal with the operating system of the plug (communications, viewing filters, power mode, eject, startup... that sort of thing), not the movements of the eva itself. Otherwise they're something convenient to hold onto while you think of the wild flailing movements you want the eva to do so that you don't have to end up swinging your limbs around in the plug.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Fri Sep 25, 2009 1:55 pm I think logically the reason for the trigger switch is that otherwise Shinji would look rather silly making a gun shape with his hand and saying "Pew-pew". So what we've established is that Eva's mimic the movements of their pilots, except when they don't, the Eva is controlled using the pilot's mind rather than their actual movements, except when they aren't, and that the buttons are there purely to control the aircon and windscreen wipers. KNOWLEDGE IS POWAH!

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Sat Sep 26, 2009 1:21 am Don't forget the pimpin' hydrolics

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Sat Sep 26, 2009 11:51 am I believe the Eva's Movement is completly dependant on the Piltos Mind/Sync Ratio. Other functions as releasing a Prog Knife, Spike Launcher, or any other goodies the eva's might have that wherent explored in the anime(Search Lights, Communications, Radio, tactical Assesment Computers, MP3 Players, Cup Holders, AC, IPod/cellphone charger, External Speakers, etc.) are controll by switches. The Controls I believe give the pilot a better grasp over control but if he/she where a bit more awesome they could simply sit down, lay back. AND CRUSH THE ANGELS WITH THERE MINDS!

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Sep 26, 2009 2:55 pm then again, as long as you've got the thought controls going would it really be that much of a bother to key the non-biological stuff to a purely mental command too? The switches could easily be completely redundant.

[]:             Quote Post by Fredrick2003 » Sun Sep 27, 2009 10:26 am Yeah, I was also under the impression that the handles were there more so that they had something to hold on to so they wouldn't be flailing their arms around in combat.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Sep 27, 2009 2:32 pm This is in a wrong subforum.

[]: Control             Quote Post by LordCaldwell » Sat Oct 03, 2009 11:19 am Basically, the point of a sync ratio is to connect your brain to the evas. That way, normaly when your brain sends signals to your hand to do stuff like clench, the A10 nerve clips (those triangles in their hair) replicat the signal and send I to the Eva. So anythng the pilot does, the Eva does. There are also buttons and stuff on the handles in the entry plug for fine control.

[]:             Quote Post by Dranzer » Sun Oct 04, 2009 2:16 pm So,all you need is concentration and a pair of A-10 Nerv clips?And what about Shinji,who can sync with the Eva without the A-10 Nerv clips?How?Is it because of Yui?

[]:             Quote Post by Soljer13 » Sun Oct 04, 2009 2:19 pm I think the A10's are only needed to refine the connection the pilots mind has with the eva's mind. Shinji being able to pilot without them is explained by Yui.
